Audits

Every review, on the record.

The audit register for One Link. We list every external review, every internal red-team, every finding (open or closed), every commit that closed it. If the page is bare, that is the truth. If it grows, that is also the truth.

External audits

None yet. This is honest, not bravado.

One Link is currently in v0.21.0-alpha. No external firm has performed a published audit of the daemon, the protocol, or this website. We have not commissioned a paid audit and the project does not yet have the legal entity required to commission one under standard engagement terms.

What that means for you, the reader, in plain language:

  • You should not trust this project at the same level as a multi-year, multi-audit project like Signal or Tor.
  • You should trust the math you can verify in your own browser tab.
  • You should trust the source you can read on GitHub.
  • You should distrust any claim on this site that is not backed by code or by a finding closed in commit history.

When an external audit lands, the firm name, the engagement scope, the findings count by severity, the report PDF, and the commits that closed each finding will all appear on this page. The audit register is signed and committed alongside the rest of the site so its history is tamper-evident via the public git log.

What an audit would cover

The scope, named in advance.

Pre-registering the scope is itself an honesty mechanism. When an audit happens, the comparison between this list and the final scope-of-work will be visible.

Cryptographic primitives

Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid signatures (ol_pqsig). X25519 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid KEM (ol_pqkem). BLAKE3 per-chunk ratchet (ol_ratchet). Shamir secret sharing (ol_threshold_recovery). Macaroon attenuation (ol_capability). Constant-time properties, side-channel resistance, KAT vector conformance, fuzz coverage.

Pairing handshake

ol_pair_qr Ed25519 invite + transcript-bound response + 5-word SAS + Levenshtein-audited wordlist. Replay resistance, cross-invite substitution, transcript swap, MITM detection, downgrade attacks.

Onion routing

ol_onion Sphinx Coherence stack: Ristretto255 blinding, ML-KEM-768 PQ-hybrid mix-in at entry hop, field-witness binding, Schnorr aggregation via Pippenger MSM. Per-hop confidentiality + integrity, traffic analysis resistance, replay protection.

Capability + folder model

Macaroon-chained capability attenuation soundness (1M-iteration gate). OR-set + LWW CRDT folder semantics (1M-iteration lattice-laws gate). Deny-by-default policy. Capability revocation atomicity.

Build + release supply chain

Reproducible-build determinism. Signed manifest verification by the Service Worker before serving cached assets. Release key custody. Build environment attestation. SBOM accuracy. Dependency audit (pip-audit + cargo audit).

Daemon attack surface

Python daemon entry points. WebRTC signaling channels. WebTransport surfaces. iOS .mobileconfig trust chain. File transfer chunking + AEAD path. Cap advertisement + acceptance. Memory safety boundaries between Python and Rust native bindings.

Website surface

This site itself: CSP enforcement, Service Worker manifest verification, the live WASM demos, the cross-tab pair-by-QR flow, the encrypted /share/ buffer, the MeshPresence Durable Object. Worker code, Durable Object code, R2 retention policy.

Privacy posture vs claims

Audit of every claim on the website against the code that backs it. Audit of every header the worker emits against the privacy promises. Audit of every byte the visitor's browser sends back to any server we operate.

Internal red-team activity

What we have done to ourselves.

In place of external audit, we publish every internal red-team finding and the commit that closed it. This is not a substitute for a real audit. It is what we can do today, honestly.

v0.21.0-alpha audit-honesty pass (2026-05-19)

Multi-agent cross-check of every claim on this website against the daemon code. Five overstatements rephrased with explicit hedges: ML-KEM-768 wire-format reserved vs active, per-chunk ratchet ready vs wired, ChunkRatchet at-rest scope, hardware-backend list, tau_c routing metaphor. Commit history.

v0.20.7 channel ratchet activation (2026-05-08)

Discovered that the Double Ratchet capability was implemented end-to-end in v0.7.2 but the daemon filtered it out of advertised CAPS, leaving channels on static AEAD. SECURITY.md T3 forward-secrecy commitment was unmet. Closed by re-adding the cap to LOCAL_CAPABILITIES advertisement.

v0.20.6 iOS trust profile (2026-05-08)

iOS Safari blocked HTTPS pair flow against self-signed daemon certs. Closed by shipping a Configuration Profile (.mobileconfig) endpoint that delivers the daemon's self-signed cert as a trusted root.

v0.20.5 multi-agent security audit (2026-05-09)

Internal 5-CRITICAL / 21-HIGH / 24-MED audit covering DTLS-Ed25519 binding, Service Worker pin trust, default-allow-all reversal, Double Ratchet activation, at-rest encryption gap. Material findings closed in v0.20.7. Lower-severity findings tracked in the issue list.

F1 fuzz gate (continuous)

Five-million-iteration fuzz across 42 binaries, zero crashes, zero false positives. Twenty-four new v2 adversarial vectors added since v0.20.5. Reproducible from cargo fuzz.

Capability + CRDT soundness gates (continuous)

One-million-iteration macaroon attenuation gate (5.94s, 0 violations). One-million-iteration OR-set + LWW lattice-laws gate (7.46s, 0 violations). Two-thousand-iteration native-mirror divergence soak (zero divergence target before authoritative cutover).

Where to look

Every claim, tied to a commit.

If you read a statement on this site and want to verify it, here is exactly where the evidence lives.

Daemon source

github.com/IamOneYouAreOneWeAreOne/one-link. Python daemon at src/one_link/. Rust native crates at native/. Tests at tests/ and per-crate tests.rs. ADR records at docs/adr/.

Website source

github.com/IamOneYouAreOneWeAreOne/one-link-website. Static HTML at dist/weareone-link.org/. Cloudflare Worker at src/worker.js. Signed manifest signer at scripts/sign-manifest.py.

CI integrity gates

Every push runs em-dash detection, personal-identifier detection, local-path detection, placeholder-token detection, timeline-language detection, manifest-freshness verification, and link-rot detection. See .github/workflows/ci.yml.

Benchmarks

Daemon: native/ol_coherence_field/BENCHMARKS.md. Onion: native/ol_onion/benches/. Capability: native/ol_capability/tests/. Reproducible via cargo bench -p <crate>.

If you want to audit

Contact welcome.

If you or your firm wants to audit any part of One Link, contact weareone@oneunity.earth with subject "audit inquiry." We will publish the engagement on this page once it begins. We do not impose NDAs that would prevent publication of findings.

If you are an individual researcher and want to report a specific vulnerability, follow the disclosure path in /.well-known/security.txt. Coordinated disclosure is honored. Bug bounty intent is documented on the /security/ page.

One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
